design_philosophy.core_loop: Place falling tetromino pieces to complete horizontal lines; cleared lines disappear and grant points
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Incremental speed increase forces player to make faster decisions as game progresses; board fills from bottom up creating spatial pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Mastery of spatial reasoning and pattern recognition; achieving high scores through sustained focus and quick reactions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Tetris' elegance lies in its constraint: one core mechanic (line clearing) that generates emergent complexity through escalating speed. No narrative, no upgrades, no shortcuts—purely mechanical skill expression. This design philosophy treats difficulty as an inherent property of the game state rather than a tunable parameter.

difficulty.continues_system: Single continuous game session; failure (board fill) ends session
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints; game runs until loss condition
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can influence game speed progression through performance (higher scores escalate speed); no manual difficulty selection documented in source

progression.unlock_mechanism: Increasing speed levels unlock organically as player clears lines; no content gates

notes: Source (TCRF) did not contain accessible game design documentation. Data derived from general knowledge of Tetris mechanics. Genesis version specifics not available in provided source material.
